name: secret-scanner
description: Detect accidentally committed secrets, credentials, and sensitive information in code.
capabilities: Scan for hardcoded API keys, tokens, and passwords
Detect cloud provider credentials (AWS, GCP, Azure)
Identify private keys and certificates in code
Analyze database connection strings for exposure
Perform entropy analysis for high-randomness strings
author: CuriousLearner
securitySummary: Pure prompt-based security documentation skill. All 207 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES - the skill documents what secrets look like for detection purposes, not actual credential access. Contains only instructions for using legitimate security scanning tools (gitleaks, truffleHog, git-secrets). No executable code, no data exfiltration, no credential exploitation.
valueStatement: Hardcoded secrets in code create serious security vulnerabilities. This skill helps you scan repositories for exposed API keys, passwords, and credentials before they become breaches.
userTitle: Detect secrets and credentials in code
limitations: This is a prompt-based skill - it does not execute external scanning tools
Requires user to run recommended tools (gitleaks, truffleHog, git-secrets) separately
Does not access or modify files without explicit user instruction
Detection accuracy depends on configured patterns and entropy thresholds

useCases.title: Pre-commit security checks
useCases.description: Scan code before commits to prevent secrets from entering version control history.

useCases.title: CI/CD pipeline security
useCases.description: Integrate secret scanning into deployment pipelines to catch credentials before production.

useCases.title: Repository security audits
useCases.description: Audit repositories before public release to ensure no credentials are exposed.

promptTemplates.title: Basic scan
promptTemplates.prompt: Scan this codebase for accidentally committed secrets. Look for AWS keys, API tokens, passwords, and private keys. List findings by severity.

promptTemplates.title: Git history scan
promptTemplates.prompt: Scan git history for secrets. Check all commits for exposed credentials and generate a report of compromised data.

promptTemplates.title: Pattern-based scan
promptTemplates.prompt: Search for Stripe, SendGrid, and Twilio API keys in the codebase. Report any matches with file locations and line numbers.

promptTemplates.title: Comprehensive audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Perform a comprehensive secret audit including regex patterns, entropy analysis, and false positive filtering. Provide a remediation report with prioritized actions.

bestPractices: Run secret scanner before every commit and before pushing to remote repositories
Integrate automated scanning in CI/CD pipelines to catch secrets before deployment
Use environment variables and secrets managers instead of hardcoding credentials
antiPatterns: Committing .env files with real credentials to version control
Ignoring secret scanner warnings to meet deadlines
Using TODO comments to mark secrets for later removal (remove them immediately instead)

faq.question: What tools does this skill use for scanning?
faq.answer: The skill provides instructions for using gitleaks, truffleHog, git-secrets, and detect-secrets. You install and run these tools separately.

faq.question: Does this skill access my files without permission?
faq.answer: No. This is a prompt-based skill that provides guidance. It cannot access files unless you explicitly request a scan.

faq.question: Can this skill remove secrets from my code?
faq.answer: No. The skill generates reports with remediation steps. You must manually remove secrets and clean git history using provided commands.

faq.question: Does this skill send data to external servers?
faq.answer: No. This skill only provides instructions. Any data transmission would come from the external scanning tools you choose to run.

faq.question: How accurate is secret detection?
faq.answer: Detection uses pattern matching and entropy analysis. False positives are possible. The skill includes guidance on distinguishing real secrets from examples.

faq.question: How does this compare to GitHub's secret scanning?
faq.answer: GitHub scans for known patterns after push. This skill provides proactive scanning before commit, including git history analysis and custom patterns.
